abstract: Constraint to access an item with either biometry or passcode.
overview:
## Discussion

Biometry doesn’t have to be available or enrolled. The item is still accessible by Touch ID even if fingers are added or removed, or by Face ID if the user is re-enrolled.

This option is equivalent to specifying [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessControlCreateFlags/biometryAny], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessControlCreateFlags/or], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessControlCreateFlags/devicePasscode].

# userPresence

**Type Property**

Constraint to access an item with either biometry or passcode.

## Declaration

```swift
static var userPresence: SecAccessControlCreateFlags { get }
```

## Discussion

Biometry doesn’t have to be available or enrolled. The item is still accessible by Touch ID even if fingers are added or removed, or by Face ID if the user is re-enrolled.

This option is equivalent to specifying [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessControlCreateFlags/biometryAny], [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessControlCreateFlags/or], and [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecAccessControlCreateFlags/devicePasscode].
title: userPresence
